subject: UX & Research
title: The Spacing Effect
description: A fast breakdown of The Spacing Effect for builders.

hook.text: Cramming before a test feels productive. But spaced repetition over days beats cramming every time.

buildup.text: Ebbinghaus found that spreading study sessions apart strengthens long-term memory far more than massing them.

discovery.text: Apps like Duolingo use this. They bring back old lessons at calculated intervals to strengthen recall.

twist.text: Onboarding that dumps all features on day one violates this. Users forget 80% by day two.

climax.text: Progressive disclosure uses spacing: reveal features gradually as users need them, not all at once.

punchline.text: Teach over time, not all at once. Spaced beats crammed.

quiz.question: Why is progressive disclosure effective for onboarding?
options: It applies the spacing effect by revealing features gradually over time
It makes the app look simpler than it actually is
Users prefer to see fewer features permanently
